channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Is Elon Musk a government employee? Here’s why it matters
item.description: Elon Musk, the world’s richest man, is CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, but when it comes to his work with the federal government, his status is a lot less clear.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: President Donald Trump has pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Domo.AI vision: Agentic workflows that create value
item.description: AI can do much more than make individual employees more productive—with a thoughtful strategy, it can transform your industry. To turn this vision into reality, businesses need AI solutions that are not only ambitious but also practical and adaptable. That’s where Domo steps in. Domo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Crenshaw pushes Apple to use 'Gulf of America' on maps
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw (R-Texas) urged Apple to officially change the name of the Gulf of Mexico on its mapping platform following President Trump’s Monday executive order declaring the land mass the Gulf of America. “Hey @tim_cook, just noticed Apple Maps still calls it the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Night Agent season 2's first five minutes have been released early, and Gabriel Basso's Peter Sutherland is forced to flee when a job goes awry
item.description: Netflix has unveiled the first few minutes from The Night Agent season 2 just two days before it's released.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Balancing the costs and opportunities of GenAI adoption
item.description: Generative AI (GenAI) is reshaping how businesses operate, offering unprecedented opportunities for greater efficiency, streamlined operations, revolutionized customer service, and enhanced decision-making.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ensuring SMBs don’t get left behind in the Gen AI wave
item.description: Don't let your SMB get left behind! Harness AI's power with expert guidance and the right tools for transformative growth.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen, an early step toward his campaign promise to dismantle what he called government “censorship” of U.S. citizens.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Can he do that? Lawyers and lawmakers debate if Trump has authority to suspend the US TikTok ban
item.description: Business leaders, lawmakers, legal scholars, and influencers who make money on TikTok are watching to see how Trump tries to resolve a thicket of regulatory, legal, financial and geopolitical issues with his signature.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to depart DOGE
item.description: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy confirmed on Monday he will not serve on President Trump’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) commission, leaving Tesla CEO Elon Musk to singularly lead the advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ramaswamy won’t serve on Trump’s government efficiency commission as he mulls run for Ohio governor
item.description: Ramaswamy, who sought the 2024 Republican nomination for president, has signaled plans to run for governor of Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Lawmakers side-eye billionaire CEOs getting prime Trump inauguration seats
item.description: Billionaire tech CEOs Jeff Bezos of Amazon, Mark Zuckerberg of Meta, Sundar Pichai of Google, Tim Cook of Apple, and Elon Musk got prime seats at President Trump’s inauguration in the Capitol rotunda — peeving lawmakers in both parties.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Army testing approach to ease communication with allies
item.description: Col. Heath Giesecke, the director of the Army’s Enterprise Cloud Management Agency, said a cloud-based commercial app is helping close a communication gap. The post Army testing approach to ease communication with allies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Build your focus stack with these proven tools and gadgets
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. A dozen tabs open. Notifications pinging. I just opened my laptop and unread emails are already calling my name.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chinese electric hypercar jumps over spikes, potholes while driving itself
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the $236,000 Yangwang U9 electric hypercar boasts 1,287 horsepower and can jump obstacles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Secure AI? Dream on, says AI red team
item.description: The group responsible for red teaming of over 100 generative AI products at Microsoft has concluded that the work of building safe and secure AI systems will never be complete.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For TikTok users, mourning, frustration and clinging to hope as TikTok ban looms
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. is inching closer and closer to a potential TikTok ban — with the nation's highest court upholding a law that's set to halt new downloads of the app starting Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 is now in a second rampdown phase, with the feature set frozen at 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok Shutdown Looms: Supreme Court Upholds Ban on Chinese-Owned App
item.description: Supreme Court votes to uphold law requiring TikTok to shut down unless sold to a US-based company, citing national security concerns
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Noem calls for ‘smaller, more nimble’ CISA during nomination hearing
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem, Trump's pick to lead DHS, criticized CISA's work on misinformation and said the agency "has gotten far off mission." The post Noem calls for ‘smaller, more nimble’ CISA during nomination hearing first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Biden’s clean AI infrastructure plan could be hanging by a thread
item.description: An executive order from outgoing US president Joe Biden aimed at advancing the nation’s leadership in AI infrastructure is “welcome and needed in theory, but this one seems rushed and somewhat at odds with the order just passed on AI chip export restrictions,” an analyst said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, Fox 5
